meta.description: Canonical application profile for Jacob Campbell

relocation.notes: Open to relocation depending on role and location

professional_summary.headline: Software Engineer focused on backend systems, full-stack delivery, and end-to-end product development.
professional_summary.summary_default: Software Engineer with experience building and maintaining production software, primarily in Java-based backend environments, with additional full-stack and AI-adjacent project work. I work end to end across solution design, estimation, development, testing, deployment, and client-facing technical communication. My background includes enterprise software for locomotive reliability, internal tooling, mobile app development, and independent side projects that emphasize practical architecture, ownership, and execution.

work_history.title: Software Engineer
bullets: Design, develop, test, and support software solutions for locomotive reliability and operational use cases.
Own work across the full development lifecycle, including requirements discussion, estimation, architecture decisions, implementation, deployment, and issue resolution.
Build backend-heavy features and integrations in a small team environment with significant individual ownership.
Contribute to AI-oriented initiatives involving sensor data, fault analysis, anomaly detection, and client-facing technical presentations.
achievements: Operated as one of the more senior backend contributors on a small development team.
Took ownership of projects initially scoped by others and drove them through implementation.
Supported AI fault prediction and sensor-analysis efforts intended to provide actionable value to clients.

work_history.title: Technical Consultant
bullets: Established the technical infrastructure for a mental healthcare practice, including website setup, appointment coordination systems, and other operational technology needs.

education.gpa_note: 4.0 semester GPA Fall 2022; exact cumulative GPA not yet confirmed
honors: UCF President's Honor Roll Certificate, Fall 2022 (4.0 GPA, 12+ credit hours)
relevant_coursework: Senior Design I & II (Pipeline to Better Placemaking)
activities: Senior Design Project: Pipeline to Better Placemaking

education.notes: Pre-UCF transfer credits including general education requirements

projects.name: AI Fault Prediction / Locomotive Sensor Analysis
projects.one_liner: Applied AI and data analysis to locomotive sensor and fault data to support earlier issue detection and client decision-making.
projects.description: Applied AI and data analysis to locomotive sensor and fault data to support earlier issue detection and client decision-making. Involved interpreting complex sensor patterns, supporting analysis and visualization, and translating technical findings into actionable insight for clients.
projects.outcome: Provided actionable value to clients through earlier fault detection capabilities.

projects.name: Pipeline to Better Placemaking
projects.one_liner: UCF Senior Design project — collaborative web application for urban placemaking research and community space analysis.
projects.description: UCF Senior Design capstone project (Fall 2022 – Spring 2023). Collaborative web application supporting urban placemaking research, enabling data collection and analysis of community spaces. Worked as part of a team across two semesters on an existing codebase.
projects.outcome: Completed two-semester capstone delivering functional application for placemaking research.

projects.name: Asobi
projects.one_liner: Full-stack movie database where users browse popular movies, create a watch list, and view available streaming services.
projects.description: Full-stack movie database application where users can browse popular movies, create a personalized watch list, and view available streaming services. Built with a Node.js backend and a frontend UI. 244 commits demonstrating sustained development.
projects.outcome: Fully functional full-stack application with 3 GitHub stars.

projects.name: AshTrail
projects.one_liner: Offline-first mobile logging and analytics app designed for structured personal tracking and long-term reliability.
projects.description: Offline-first mobile logging and analytics app designed for structured personal tracking and long-term reliability. Built from concept through architecture and implementation with emphasis on data persistence and user experience.
projects.outcome: Functional personal tracking tool with offline-first architecture.

projects.name: TourReporter
projects.one_liner: iOS application built in Swift with CocoaPods dependency management.
projects.description: Native iOS application built in Swift using Xcode and CocoaPods for dependency management. Demonstrates mobile development skills and iOS platform familiarity.

projects.name: Flix
projects.one_liner: Netflix-like mobile application and initial attempt at the Asobi mobile experience.
projects.description: Netflix-like iOS mobile application serving as an initial attempt at the Asobi concept in a native mobile format. Built in Swift.

projects.name: Minecraft Modding
projects.one_liner: Custom Minecraft mod built in Java, demonstrating Java proficiency and plugin architecture.
projects.description: Custom Minecraft mod built in Java. Demonstrates Java proficiency, working with existing APIs and plugin architectures, and personal-project initiative.

projects.name: Wedding Website
projects.one_liner: Self-built wedding website tailored to personal event needs and user experience goals.
projects.description: Self-built wedding website tailored to personal event needs and user experience goals. Demonstrated end-to-end web development from design through deployment.

projects.name: Personal iOS Apps
projects.one_liner: Built personal-use iOS applications to solve specific day-to-day needs.
projects.description: Built personal-use iOS applications for yourself and your fiancée to solve specific day-to-day needs. Demonstrated initiative, mobile development skills, and practical problem-solving.

projects.name: Mental Healthcare Practice Technical Setup
projects.one_liner: Established technical infrastructure for a mental healthcare practice including website, appointment coordination, and operational systems.
projects.description: Set up the complete technical infrastructure for a sister's mental healthcare practice, including website development, appointment coordination systems, and other operational technology needs.
projects.outcome: Fully operational technical setup enabling the practice to serve clients.

compensation.salary_flexibility_phrasing: I'm targeting roles in the $110,000 to $120,000 range, though I'm open to discussing the full compensation package depending on the role, scope, and growth opportunity.
compensation.notice_period_notes: Assumed standard 2 weeks; pending offer letter confirmation

cover_letter.structure: Opening with role/company fit; current experience and ownership; 1-2 relevant projects; closing on enthusiasm and fit.
